title: lora_l2.c: k_thread_join timeout sequence comment doesn't explain the 100+5000ms strategy
description:
Location: lora_l2.c:329-341

The stop() function joins with a 100ms timeout, then RX_TIMEOUT_MS (5000ms), then aborts. The comment says 'short initial timeout for the common case where the thread exits quickly'.

What goes wrong: Why 100ms specifically? Is it based on lora_recv() internal timing, expected worst-case for the running-flag check, or arbitrary? Without this context, a future maintainer might change it to 50ms (faster shutdown!) or 500ms (more margin!) without understanding the tradeoff.

Suggested fix: Add one sentence: '100ms is ample time for a thread that checked running between lora_recv() calls; if still blocked, it's inside lora_recv() and needs up to RX_TIMEOUT_MS to return.'
close_reason: Fixed: Added clarifying sentence explaining why 100ms is used as the initial timeout (ample time for thread checking running flag between lora_recv() calls; if blocked inside lora_recv() needs up to RX_TIMEOUT_MS)
